sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings (ASHE) — Ethnicity Pay Gap

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Labour Force Survey — Unemployment by Ethnicity

metadata.methodology: The ethnic pay gap is the percentage difference in median hourly earnings between ethnic minority workers and white British workers, from the Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings. Unemployment ratio is Black unemployment rate divided by white unemployment rate from the Labour Force Survey.
knownIssues: Ethnic pay gap estimates vary significantly depending on how ethnic groups are aggregated
Small sample sizes for some ethnic groups mean estimates have wide confidence intervals
The Labour Force Survey changed methodology in 2020 from face-to-face to telephone interviews, creating a series break
Wealth data by ethnicity is only available biennially and has limited geographic granularity
Self-reported ethnicity classifications changed between Census 2011 and Census 2021
